walkthrough.title: Lay Out the Location Tiles
walkthrough.text: Let's get Istanbul set up. Find the 16 location tiles. These are square tiles, each showing a different place in the bazaar. For your first game, arrange them in the four-by-four grid layout recommended in the rulebook. For experienced players, you can randomize the tile placement for a fresh experience every time.

walkthrough.title: Identify the Key Locations
walkthrough.text: Let's get familiar with what is on the board. You will see the Fountain in the center, warehouses for fabric, spices, and fruit, the Wainwright for upgrades, two markets for selling goods, two mosques for abilities, the Sultan's Palace and Gemstone Dealer for rubies, and several other locations. Do not worry about memorizing them all right now.

walkthrough.title: Place the Governor and Smuggler
walkthrough.text: Find the two neutral pawns labeled Governor and Smuggler. Place both of them on the Police Station tile. These characters will move around the board during the game, and you can interact with them when you cross their path.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Markets
walkthrough.text: Find the demand tiles for the Small Market and Large Market. Randomly place one demand tile on each market location. These tiles show which combinations of goods buyers want and how much Lira they will pay. The demand changes throughout the game as players sell goods.

walkthrough.title: Place the Mosque Tiles
walkthrough.text: Find the small ability tiles for the mosques. Place two ability tiles on the Small Mosque and two on the Great Mosque, face up so everyone can see what they do. These tiles grant permanent abilities to players who purchase them during the game.

walkthrough.title: Distribute the Rubies
walkthrough.text: Place ruby gemstones on the Sultan's Palace, Gemstone Dealer, Small Mosque, and Great Mosque as indicated on each tile. Rubies are the victory objective, so keep an eye on where they are available throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Coin Supply
walkthrough.text: Place all the Lira coins in a shared supply near the board where everyone can reach them. Lira is the currency you will use to buy rubies, wheelbarrow upgrades, and other things throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Prepare Bonus Cards
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the deck of bonus cards and place it face down near the board. You will draw these at the Caravansary location during the game. They provide powerful one-time effects like free goods, extra movement, or wild goods at the Sultan's Palace.

walkthrough.title: Build Your Merchant Stack
walkthrough.text: Each player picks a color and takes their merchant disc and four assistant discs. Stack your large merchant disc on top of your four smaller assistant discs to form a single stack. This stack is how you move around the bazaar, dropping off and picking up assistants as you go.

walkthrough.title: Place Stacks on the Fountain
walkthrough.text: Everyone places their entire merchant stack on the Fountain tile. All players begin here. The Fountain is your home base and the place you will return to whenever you need to recall all of your assistants.

walkthrough.title: Take Your Wheelbarrow
walkthrough.text: Each player takes one wheelbarrow tile. This shows your goods capacity. You start with room for two of each good type: fabric, spices, fruit, and jewelry. You begin with zero goods on your wheelbarrow. You will fill it up by visiting warehouse locations.

walkthrough.title: Collect Starting Lira
walkthrough.text: Each player receives starting Lira based on turn order. The first player gets two Lira, the second player gets three Lira, the third gets four, and so on. This small bonus compensates later players for going after the first player.

walkthrough.title: Place Family Members
walkthrough.text: Each player takes their family member pawn and places it on the Police Station tile. Your family member is a useful tool that lets you perform remote actions at distant locations without moving your merchant there.

walkthrough.title: Choose First Player
walkthrough.text: Pick a random person to go first. Play proceeds clockwise. The goal is to be the first player to collect five rubies. Once any player reaches five rubies, that player wins immediately. Ready to explore the bazaar? Let's learn the rules.

First game: use recommended layout; experienced: randomize

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how a turn works in Istanbul. On your turn, you do four things in order. First, move your merchant stack one or two spaces. Second, drop off or pick up an assistant. Third, perform the location's action. Fourth, handle any encounters with other merchants or neutral pawns. The game flows quickly once you get the rhythm.

walkthrough.title: Movement Rules
walkthrough.text: You must move your merchant stack one or two spaces in any orthogonal direction. That means up, down, left, or right, but not diagonally. You cannot stay in place. You are allowed to pass through or stop on tiles occupied by other players. Moving exactly two spaces is often more efficient, so plan your routes carefully.

walkthrough.title: The Assistant System
walkthrough.text: This is the heart of Istanbul. When you arrive at a location, you must either leave one assistant from your stack or pick up one of your assistants already there. If you leave an assistant, your stack gets shorter. If you pick one up, your stack grows. You need at least one assistant in your stack to take most location actions, so managing your assistants is critical.

walkthrough.title: The Fountain
walkthrough.text: The Fountain is your reset button. When you move to the Fountain, you immediately recall all of your assistants from everywhere on the board back into your stack. You do not need an assistant already there to use it. Plan your routes so that when you run low on assistants, you swing by the Fountain to pick everyone up.

walkthrough.title: Warehouse Locations
walkthrough.text: There are three warehouses on the board: Fabric Warehouse, Spice Warehouse, and Fruit Warehouse. When you visit a warehouse, you fill your wheelbarrow with that good type up to your maximum capacity. This is free and does not cost any Lira. Warehouses are your primary way to stock up on goods for selling or delivering.

walkthrough.title: Selling at the Markets
walkthrough.text: The Small Market and Large Market let you sell goods for Lira. Each market has a demand tile showing which combination of goods the buyers want and how much they will pay. After you sell, you roll a die to set the new demand. Markets are your main source of income for buying rubies and upgrades.

walkthrough.title: The Wainwright
walkthrough.text: Visit the Wainwright to upgrade your wheelbarrow. Pay seven Lira to add one extension, which increases your capacity for every good type by one. You can buy up to three extensions total. After purchasing your third extension, you earn one ruby. Upgrading early lets you carry more goods for the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: The Sultan's Palace
walkthrough.text: The Sultan's Palace is one of the most important locations. You deliver a specific combination of goods to receive one ruby. The cost in goods increases with each ruby taken from the Palace. Early visits are cheaper, so delivering to the Sultan sooner rather than later gives you a better deal.

walkthrough.title: The Gemstone Dealer
walkthrough.text: The Gemstone Dealer lets you buy rubies with pure Lira. The first ruby costs 12 Lira, and the price goes up by one Lira for each subsequent ruby purchased by any player. This is a straightforward ruby source that does not require any goods, just cash.

walkthrough.title: The Mosques
walkthrough.text: The Small Mosque and Great Mosque each have two ability tiles you can purchase by paying specific goods. These tiles grant permanent abilities for the rest of the game, like rerolling dice or gaining extra resources. When you collect both tiles from either mosque, you also earn one ruby.

walkthrough.title: Other Key Locations
walkthrough.text: Let's cover the remaining locations. The Post Office gives you a fixed set of goods and Lira. The Caravansary lets you draw two bonus cards and keep one. The Tea House lets you name a number and gamble for Lira. The Black Market gives you one good of your choice plus a bonus die roll. Each location has its own rhythm, and you will learn which ones fit your strategy as you play.

walkthrough.title: The Police Station and Family Member
walkthrough.text: From the Police Station, you can send your family member pawn to any location on the board to perform that location's action remotely. Your family member stays at that location until another player's merchant lands on it. When that happens, they pay you two Lira and your family member returns to the Police Station.

walkthrough.title: Governor and Smuggler
walkthrough.text: If you land on a tile with the Governor, you may pay two Lira to gain one bonus card or take one good of your choice. If you land on the Smuggler, you may pay two Lira to gain one good of your choice. After interacting with either, you move that pawn to any other tile on the board.

walkthrough.title: Encountering Other Merchants
walkthrough.text: When you land on a tile where another player's merchant disc is present, you must pay that player two Lira. If you cannot afford to pay, you still complete your action but must pay the Lira later. This creates interesting blocking dynamics, so be aware of where your opponents are moving.

walkthrough.title: Bonus Cards
walkthrough.text: Bonus cards are gained at the Caravansary. They provide powerful one-time effects like gaining free goods, extra movement, bonus Lira, or acting as wild goods when delivering to the Sultan's Palace. You can also earn one ruby by collecting five bonus cards. Use them strategically to save turns or stretch your resources.

walkthrough.title: Ruby Sources Overview
walkthrough.text: There are six ways to earn rubies. Deliver goods to the Sultan's Palace. Buy rubies at the Gemstone Dealer. Complete all three wheelbarrow extensions at the Wainwright. Collect both tiles from the Small Mosque. Collect both tiles from the Great Mosque. And collect five bonus cards. You need five rubies to win, so diversify your approach.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: The game ends immediately when any player collects their fifth ruby. That player wins. There is no final round. If two players would reach five rubies in the same round, the player who achieved it earlier in turn order wins. Tiebreakers go to most Lira, then most goods.

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Bazaar
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of Istanbul! In this practice tutorial, we will walk through several turns with three example players to show you how the game flows. Everyone starts at the Fountain with a full stack of four assistants. Let's see how a typical opening plays out.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Move to the Wainwright
walkthrough.text: Player 1 moves two spaces from the Fountain to the Wainwright. They drop off one assistant and pay seven Lira to purchase their first wheelbarrow extension. Their capacity for every good type just went from two to three. Smart opening move because bigger capacity pays off for the entire rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: Why Upgrading Early Matters
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about why that was a strong first move. Every time Player 1 visits a warehouse from now on, they collect three goods instead of two. Over the course of the game, that one extra good per visit adds up enormously. Think of the Wainwright as an investment that compounds over time.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: Move to the Fabric Warehouse
walkthrough.text: Player 2 moves two spaces to the Fabric Warehouse, drops off an assistant, and fills their wheelbarrow with fabric up to their maximum capacity of two. They are planning to sell these goods at a market or deliver them to the Sultan's Palace later. Loading up on goods early gives you options.

walkthrough.title: Player 3: The First Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 3 sees the Gemstone Dealer and gets excited about buying a ruby right away. They move there, drop off an assistant, but realize the first ruby costs 12 Lira and they only have four. They wasted a turn and an assistant on a location they cannot use yet. The action fizzles and they accomplished nothing.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Know What You Can Afford
walkthrough.text: Before you move, always check the cost of the action at your destination. Moving to a location you cannot afford wastes both your turn and an assistant. Plan one or two turns ahead so every move accomplishes something concrete.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Visit the Spice Warehouse
walkthrough.text: Player 1 moves one space to the Spice Warehouse and drops off another assistant. They fill their upgraded wheelbarrow with three spices. Notice how the wheelbarrow extension is already paying off. Player 1 now has a full load of spices ready for the Sultan's Palace or a market.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: Move to the Small Market
walkthrough.text: Player 2 moves to the Small Market, drops off an assistant, and sells their fabric for Lira. The demand tile shows a good payout. After selling, they roll the demand die to set new prices. Player 2 now has a nice stack of Lira to use for upgrades or buying rubies later.

walkthrough.title: Player 3: Recovery at the Fruit Warehouse
walkthrough.text: Player 3 learns from their mistake and moves to the Fruit Warehouse instead. They drop off an assistant and load up on fruit. No Lira wasted, and they now have goods to work with. Sometimes the best move after a mistake is a simple, productive action.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Deliver to the Sultan's Palace
walkthrough.text: Player 1 moves to the Sultan's Palace and delivers their goods to earn a ruby. The first delivery is the cheapest, requiring fewer goods. Player 1 cashes in their spices and collects a ruby. That is one of five they need to win.

walkthrough.title: The Assistant Problem
walkthrough.text: Let's check in on everyone's assistant situation. Player 1 has been dropping off assistants at every stop and only has one left in their stack. Player 2 has two remaining. Player 3 has two as well. When your stack runs out, you need to head to the Fountain to recall everyone. Planning when to visit the Fountain is a critical part of the game.

walkthrough.title: Player 2: The Second Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 2 wants to visit the Sultan's Palace but it is three spaces away. They can only move one or two spaces per turn. Instead of finding a productive intermediate stop, they move to an empty tile where they accomplish nothing useful. They burned a turn just getting closer to their destination.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Make Every Stop Count
walkthrough.text: Never waste a move on a location that does nothing for you. Always have a backup plan for each tile you might pass through. Even if your ideal destination is far away, stop at a warehouse or the Post Office along the way. Every turn should accomplish something, even if it is small.

walkthrough.title: Player 1: Head to the Fountain
walkthrough.text: Player 1 is down to their last assistant and heads to the Fountain. They recall all four assistants from across the board back into their stack. Now Player 1 is fully restocked and ready for another loop around the bazaar. This is the natural rhythm of the game: deploy assistants, take actions, return to Fountain, repeat.

walkthrough.title: Player 3: The Third Mistake
walkthrough.text: Player 3 decides to visit the Great Mosque to grab an ability tile. They arrive and see the tile costs two fabric. But Player 3 only has fruit on their wheelbarrow, not fabric. Once again, they moved to a location without checking that they had the right resources. Another wasted assistant and turn.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Match Your Resources to Your Destination
walkthrough.text: Before moving, check what resources are on your wheelbarrow and what your destination requires. The mosques want specific goods. The Sultan's Palace wants specific combinations. The markets want specific sets. Always verify you have what is needed before you commit to a move.

walkthrough.title: Building a Route
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about efficient routing. The best players plan a loop of three to five stops before heading back to the Fountain. For example: Fountain, then warehouse, then warehouse, then Sultan's Palace or market, then mosque, then back to the Fountain. Each stop in the loop accomplishes something and flows naturally into the next.

walkthrough.title: Using the Family Member
walkthrough.text: Player 2 visits the Police Station and sends their family member to the Spice Warehouse. The family member performs the warehouse action remotely, filling Player 2's wheelbarrow with spices. This is a powerful trick because it lets you take an action at a faraway location without actually moving there.

walkthrough.title: Encountering the Governor
walkthrough.text: Player 3 moves to a location where the Governor happens to be. After performing the location action, they pay two Lira to interact with the Governor and gain one good of their choice. Then they move the Governor to another tile. Encounters with the Governor and Smuggler are small bonuses that can give you a useful edge.

walkthrough.title: Mid-Game Check
walkthrough.text: Let's take stock. Player 1 has one ruby and a fully restocked assistant stack. Player 2 has solid Lira income and goods ready to deliver. Player 3 made some mistakes but is learning and recovering. Everyone is starting to see their preferred ruby sources. The race is heating up.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations! You have walked through the core flow of Istanbul. You have seen how to move and manage assistants, visit warehouses and markets, deliver goods for rubies, use the Fountain to reset, and interact with the Governor. You have also seen three common beginner mistakes: going to locations you cannot afford, wasting moves on unproductive stops, and not matching your resources to your destination. You are ready to play a full game.

walkthrough.title: Before You Trade
walkthrough.text:
Before you dive into the bazaar, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and route-planning advice.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to every location and game term.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Before we talk strategy, here are the biggest beginner pitfalls. First, running out of assistants with no plan to reach the Fountain. Second, visiting locations without checking that you can afford the action. Third, ignoring the Gemstone Dealer because buying rubies for Lira feels too simple. Fourth, tunnel-visioning on one ruby source instead of diversifying across multiple sources. Keep these in mind and you will avoid the most common traps.

walkthrough.title: Upgrade Your Wheelbarrow Early
walkthrough.text: One of the strongest openings in Istanbul is heading to the Wainwright on your first or second turn. Each extension increases your capacity for every good type, which means every warehouse visit becomes more productive for the rest of the game. After three extensions you also earn a ruby. Think of it as an investment that pays dividends on every future warehouse trip.

walkthrough.title: Plan Your Routes
walkthrough.text: Istanbul rewards efficient movement more than anything else. Before your turn, look at the board and plan a loop of three to five productive stops before you need to return to the Fountain. Every stop should accomplish something. If your ideal destination is three spaces away, find a useful intermediate stop along the way. Wasted turns are the biggest competitive disadvantage in this game.

walkthrough.title: Assistant Management
walkthrough.text: Your assistants are your most limited resource. You start with four, and you need to leave one behind at every stop. That means you can make about four productive stops before needing to visit the Fountain. The trick is to plan your route so that you pick up previously dropped assistants along the way, extending your trips. A Great Mosque ability tile can also help by returning an assistant to your stack.

walkthrough.title: Diversify Your Ruby Sources
walkthrough.text: You need five rubies to win, and there are six sources. Trying to get all five from one or two sources is usually slower than spreading across three or four. A balanced approach might be one from the Wainwright, one from the Sultan's Palace, one from the Gemstone Dealer, and two from the mosques. Adapt based on what other players are doing and what the board layout makes efficient.

walkthrough.title: Use Your Family Member
walkthrough.text: Many new players forget about the family member entirely. It is essentially a free remote action at any location on the board. Send it to a location that is far from your current route. Great targets include warehouses, the Post Office, or even the Sultan's Palace if you have the goods. It also earns you two Lira when another player bumps into it, which is a nice bonus.

walkthrough.title: Mosque Tiles Are Powerful
walkthrough.text: The ability tiles at the mosques are permanent upgrades that benefit you for the rest of the game. Prioritize grabbing mosque tiles early because their value compounds over many turns. The extra die reroll, bonus goods at the Fountain, or assistant return abilities can save you entire turns of wasted movement. Plus, collecting both tiles from one mosque earns you a ruby.

walkthrough.title: Watch Your Opponents
walkthrough.text: Istanbul is a race, and awareness of your opponents matters. Pay attention to how many rubies other players have and which sources they are targeting. If someone is one ruby away from winning, you may need to adjust your plan and rush your own fifth ruby. Also watch where opponents' merchants are. Landing on their merchant costs you two Lira, so route around them when possible.

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Don't run out of assistants without a Fountain plan
Don't visit locations you can't afford
Don't ignore the Gemstone Dealer — buying rubies for Lira is valid
Don't tunnel-vision on 1 ruby source; diversify

bullets: Permanent abilities that compound over many turns
Grab early for maximum benefit
Die reroll, bonus goods, assistant return = save entire turns
Both tiles from 1 mosque = 1 ruby

summary.title: Watch Your Opponents
bullets: Track opponents' ruby count and target sources
If someone is 1 ruby from winning, rush your own 5th
Landing on opponents' merchants costs 2 Lira
Route around opponents when possible

walkthrough.title: The Wainwright Rush
walkthrough.text: One of the most consistent strategies is to rush all three wheelbarrow extensions as fast as possible. This costs 21 Lira total but gives you maximum carrying capacity and one ruby. With a fully upgraded wheelbarrow, every warehouse visit fills you with five goods instead of two. This makes Sultan's Palace deliveries and market sales far more efficient for the rest of the game.

walkthrough.title: The Sultan's Palace Focus
walkthrough.text: The Sultan's Palace rewards players who deliver early, because the goods cost increases with each ruby taken. If you can be the first to deliver, you get the cheapest deal. Pair this with a Wainwright extension or two so you can carry enough goods in a single trip. The ideal combo is warehouse, warehouse, Sultan's Palace in three consecutive turns.

walkthrough.title: The Lira Engine
walkthrough.text: An alternative approach focuses on generating massive Lira income through the markets and Tea House. Sell goods at the Large Market for high payouts, use the Tea House for bonus Lira, and funnel all that cash into the Gemstone Dealer. This strategy bypasses the need for specific goods at the Sultan's Palace and lets you buy rubies with pure money. It works especially well when the board layout puts markets near warehouses.

walkthrough.title: Mosque Tile Priority
walkthrough.text: Experienced players know that certain mosque tiles are game-changing. The tile that returns an assistant to your stack effectively gives you an extra stop every cycle. The tile that grants bonus goods when you visit the Fountain turns your reset trip into a productive action. Identify the strongest tiles early and prioritize them before opponents snap them up. Two rubies from two mosques is achievable with good routing.

walkthrough.title: Route Optimization
walkthrough.text: The highest-level skill in Istanbul is route optimization. Study the board layout at the start of each game and identify tight loops that let you accomplish the most with the fewest moves. The ideal loop hits a warehouse, a ruby source, and flows back toward the Fountain in four or five moves. Also look for spots where you can pick up previously dropped assistants to extend your trips without visiting the Fountain.

walkthrough.title: Blocking and Interaction
walkthrough.text: In competitive games, positioning matters. Parking your merchant on a location your opponent needs forces them to pay you two Lira. Sending your family member to a popular location earns you two Lira every time an opponent visits. Moving the Governor or Smuggler to a tile your opponent wants can deny them a useful interaction. These small economic advantages add up and can determine who gets their fifth ruby first.

walkthrough.title: Bonus Card Strategy
walkthrough.text: Bonus cards are often undervalued by newer players. Five bonus cards earn you one ruby, and the cards themselves provide powerful one-time effects. Wild goods cards let you deliver to the Sultan's Palace without having the exact goods. Extra movement cards let you reach distant locations in a single turn. If the Caravansary is conveniently located near your route, picking up bonus cards alongside your main strategy is a low-cost way to earn an extra ruby.

walkthrough.title: Reading the Board Layout
walkthrough.text: Every randomized board layout creates different optimal strategies. Before the game starts, scan the grid for clusters of synergistic locations. Are the warehouses near the Sultan's Palace? That favors a goods delivery strategy. Are the markets near the Gemstone Dealer? The Lira engine becomes stronger. Is the Fountain centrally located? Routes become more flexible. The best players adapt their strategy to the specific board layout rather than forcing the same plan every game.

entries.definition: Your large disc that represents your trader moving through the bazaar. The merchant sits on top of your assistant stack and must move one or two spaces orthogonally each turn.
Small discs stacked beneath your merchant. You must leave one behind or pick one up at each location you visit. Without assistants, you cannot take most actions. You start with four assistants.
Your personal tile tracking goods capacity. Starts at two of each good type. Can be upgraded three times at the Wainwright, reaching a maximum capacity of five per good type.
An upgrade tile purchased at the Wainwright for seven Lira. Each extension adds one capacity for every good type. After the third extension, you earn one ruby.
Gemstone tokens representing the victory objective. The first player to collect five rubies wins the game immediately. Rubies can be obtained from six different sources.
The currency of Istanbul. Used to buy wheelbarrow extensions, rubies at the Gemstone Dealer, and interact with the Governor and Smuggler. Earned by selling goods at markets and from the Tea House.

Resources carried on your wheelbarrow: fabric, spices, fruit, and jewelry. Collected from warehouses and the Black Market. Used for deliveries at the Sultan's Palace, purchases at mosques, and sales at markets.
One-time use cards drawn at the Caravansary. Provide effects like free goods, extra movement, bonus Lira, or wild goods at the Sultan's Palace. Collecting five bonus cards earns one ruby.
Tiles placed on the Small Market and Large Market showing which goods combinations buyers want and how much Lira they pay. Demand changes after each sale when the die is rolled.
Ability tiles purchased at the Small Mosque and Great Mosque. Grant permanent abilities for the rest of the game. Collecting both tiles from one mosque earns one ruby.

A neutral pawn sent from the Police Station to perform remote actions at any location. Stays at the target location until another merchant lands on it, at which point that merchant pays two Lira and the family member returns to the Police Station.

A neutral pawn that moves around the board. When you land on the Governor's tile, you may pay two Lira to gain one bonus card or one good of your choice. After interacting, move the Governor to any other tile.
A neutral pawn that moves around the board. When you land on the Smuggler's tile, you may pay two Lira to gain one good of your choice. After interacting, move the Smuggler to any other tile.
The central location tile. Moving here recalls all your assistants from the entire board back into your stack. No assistant is needed to use the Fountain. All players start here.
A warehouse location. Visiting fills your wheelbarrow with fabric up to your maximum capacity at no cost.
A warehouse location. Visiting fills your wheelbarrow with spices up to your maximum capacity at no cost.
A warehouse location. Visiting fills your wheelbarrow with fruit up to your maximum capacity at no cost.
A market location where you sell a specific combination of goods for Lira according to the current demand tile. After selling, roll the demand die to set new prices.
A market location where you sell a specific combination of goods for Lira. Typically offers higher payouts than the Small Market for more complex goods combinations.
The upgrade location. Pay seven Lira to add one wheelbarrow extension, increasing capacity for all good types by one. After three extensions, earn one ruby.
A ruby source. Deliver a specific combination of goods to earn one ruby. The goods cost increases with each ruby taken from the Palace. Early deliveries are cheaper.
A ruby source. Buy one ruby for Lira. The first ruby costs 12 Lira, and the price increases by one Lira for each subsequent ruby purchased by any player.
A location with two ability tiles purchasable with specific goods. Tiles grant permanent abilities. Collecting both tiles from the Small Mosque earns one ruby.
A location with two ability tiles purchasable with specific goods. Tiles grant permanent abilities. Collecting both tiles from the Great Mosque earns one ruby.
A location that grants a fixed set of goods and Lira. The payout follows a cycle that resets, giving different combinations each visit.
A location where you draw two bonus cards from the deck and keep one. Bonus cards provide powerful one-time effects and collecting five earns a ruby.

A location where you name a number between three and 12, then another player rolls two dice. If the roll meets or exceeds your number, you gain that many Lira. Higher numbers are riskier but more rewarding.
A location where you gain one good of your choice, then roll dice for a chance at a bonus good. Also allows selling blue jewelry.
The location where you send your family member to any tile on the board to perform that tile's action remotely. All family members start here and return here after being bumped.
Movement in straight lines: up, down, left, or right. Diagonal movement is not allowed in Istanbul. You must move exactly one or two spaces per turn.
When your merchant lands on a tile with another player's merchant, you must pay them two Lira. This also applies when landing on the Governor or Smuggler, who offer optional paid interactions.
One of the four good types. Collected at the Fabric Warehouse. Used for deliveries at Sultan's Palace, mosque tile purchases, and market sales. Represented by a colored token on your wheelbarrow.
One of the four good types. Collected at the Spice Warehouse. Used for deliveries, mosque purchases, and market sales.
One of the four good types. Collected at the Fruit Warehouse. Used for deliveries, mosque purchases, and market sales.

The fourth good type, also called blue goods. Not collected from a standard warehouse. Gained from the Black Market or certain bonus cards. Used for Sultan's Palace deliveries and can be sold at the Black Market.
